user_message: The CSA escalated Pacific Northwest Outfitters to me -- can you draft the follow-up?

input_artifacts.content:
From: Andrew Taylor <andrew.taylor@pacificnorth.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-28 10:48 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Pacific Northwest Outfitters partner engagement

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with partner engagement. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Andrew Taylor <andrew.taylor@pacificnorth.com>
Date: 2024-02-19 15:22 EST
Subject: Re: partner engagement Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about partner engagement. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Andrew Taylor <andrew.taylor@pacificnorth.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-04 15:35 EST
Subject: Re: Re: partner engagement Timeline

Just checking in on the partner engagement proposal. Our IT Director is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: partner engagement Discussion - Pacific Northwest Outfitters
Date: 2024-03-02
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft), Michelle (Pacific Northwest Outfitters)

[00:01:30] Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into partner engagement.

[00:06:00] Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:08:00] Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:12:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the partner engagement front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

[00:14:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): The timeline for security compliance is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:19:00] Michelle (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): On the partner engagement front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:24:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:28:00] Michelle (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): On the partner engagement front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

[00:31:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the partner engagement front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

[00:33:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about cost optimization. Can we address those?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-M trap (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-M failure mode (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct partner engagement decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-M -- Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
